en.content: The book ‘The Origin of Species by Means of Natural Selection’ was authored by

question: The optimum pH at which the enzymes pepsin and trypsin of the human alimentary canal works are

en.content: The optimum pH at which the enzymes pepsin and trypsin of the human alimentary canal works are
en.explanation:
The correct answer is Option 1: pH $2.0$ and $7.9$, respectively.

Step-by-step:

  1. Pepsin works in the stomach.

  • The stomach has a highly acidic medium due to hydrochloric acid.

  • So, pepsin works best at about pH $2.0$.

  1. Trypsin works in the small intestine.

  • The small intestine has an alkaline medium.

  • So, trypsin works best at about pH $7.9$.

Therefore,

$$ \text{Pepsin} \rightarrow pH\,2.0,\qquad \text{Trypsin} \rightarrow pH\,7.9 $$

So, the answer is Option 1.

question: The amount of air breathed in and out during a normal respiration by human lungs is referred to as

en.content: The amount of air breathed in and out during a normal respiration by human lungs is referred to as
en.explanation:
The correct answer is Option 2: Tidal volume.

Here is a step-by-step explanation of the terms based on the NCERT textbook to help you understand why this is the correct answer:

1. Tidal Volume (TV)

According to your textbook, Tidal Volume is defined as the volume of air inspired or expired during a normal respiration. For a healthy man, this volume is approximately $500 \text{ mL}$ per inspiration or expiration. Therefore, this matches the description given in your question perfectly.

Let us also look at the other options so you can understand what they mean:

2. Vital Capacity (VC)

This is the maximum volume of air a person can breathe in after a forced expiration. It includes the Expiratory Reserve Volume (ERV), Tidal Volume (TV), and Inspiratory Reserve Volume (IRV). It is not the volume during normal respiration.

3. Inspiratory Reserve Volume (IRV)

This refers to the additional volume of air a person can inspire by a forcible inspiration, over and above the normal Tidal Volume. It averages $2500 \text{ mL}$ to $3000 \text{ mL}$.

4. Total Lung Capacity (TLC)

This is the total volume of air accommodated in the lungs at the end of a forced inspiration. It includes Residual Volume (RV), Expiratory Reserve Volume (ERV), Tidal Volume (TV), and Inspiratory Reserve Volume (IRV).

Since the question specifically asks for the amount of air breathed in and out during normal respiration, Tidal volume is the correct biological term.

hi.content: मानव फेफड़ों (फुप्फुस ) द्वारा सामान्य श्वसन के दौरान प्रति श्वास बायु को अंदर खींचने और वायु को बाहर मुक्त करने की मात्रा को किस रूप में व्यक्त किया जाता है ?
hi.explanation:
सही उत्तर है विकल्प 2: आवृत्ति आयतन (Tidal volume)।

यहाँ आपके एनसीईआरटी पाठ्यपुस्तक के आधार पर इन शब्दों की चरण-दर-चरण व्याख्या प्रस्तुत है, जिससे आपको समझने में मदद मिलेगी कि यह उत्तर क्यों सही है:

1. आवृत्ति आयतन (Tidal Volume - TV)

आपकी पाठ्यपुस्तक के अनुसार, आवृत्ति आयतन वह वायु आयतन है, जो सामान्य श्वसन के दौरान अंदर लिया या बाहर निकाला जाता है। एक स्वस्थ पुरुष में यह मात्रा लगभग $500 \text{ mL}$ प्रति श्वसन होती है। अतः यह आपके प्रश्न में दी गई परिभाषा से पूरी तरह मेल खाती है।

आइए अन्य विकल्पों को भी देखें ताकि आप समझ सकें कि वे क्या अर्थ रखते हैं:

2. जीवन क्षमता (Vital Capacity - VC)

यह वायु की अधिकतम मात्रा है, जिसे कोई व्यक्ति जबर्दस्ती श्वसन के बाद अंदर ले सकता है। इसमें निष्कासनीय संचित आयतन (Expiratory Reserve Volume - ERV), आवृत्ति आयतन (TV) एवं प्रेरणीय संचित आयतन (Inspiratory Reserve Volume - IRV) सम्मिलित होते हैं। यह सामान्य श्वसन के दौरान वायु की मात्रा नहीं है।

3. प्रेरणीय संचित आयतन (Inspiratory Reserve Volume - IRV)

यह अतिरिक्त वायु आयतन है, जिसे कोई व्यक्ति सामान्य आवृत्ति आयतन के अतिरिक्त, जबर्दस्ती श्वसन द्वारा अंदर ले सकता है। इसका औसत $2500 \text{ mL}$ से $3000 \text{ mL}$ होता है।

4. कुल फुफ्फुसीय क्षमता (Total Lung Capacity - TLC)

यह फेफड़ों में जबरदस्ती श्वसन के अंत में समाहित वायु का कुल आयतन है। इसमें अवशिष्ट आयतन (Residual Volume - RV), निष्कासनीय संचित आयतन (ERV), आवृत्ति आयतन (TV) एवं प्रेरणीय संचित आयतन (IRV) सम्मिलित होते हैं।

चूँकि प्रश्न में सामान्य श्वसन के दौरान अंदर ली या बाहर निकाली गई वायु की मात्रा पूछी गई है, इसलिए जैविक रूप से सही शब्द "आवृत्ति आयतन" (Tidal Volume) है।

options: Arteries carry blood away from heart while veins bring blood towards the heart
Pulmonary arteries always carry oxygenated blood while Pulmonary veins always carry deoxygenated blood
Arteries have thicker and more elastic walls compared to veins in general
Arteries do not have valves but veins do have valves
question: Which one among the following statements about arteries and veins in humans is NOT correct ?

en.content: Which one among the following statements about arteries and veins in humans is NOT correct ?

options.content: Arteries carry blood away from heart while veins bring blood towards the heart
Pulmonary arteries always carry oxygenated blood while Pulmonary veins always carry deoxygenated blood
Arteries have thicker and more elastic walls compared to veins in general
Arteries do not have valves but veins do have valves

en.explanation:
Pulmonary arteries always carry oxygenated blood while pulmonary veins always carry deoxygenated blood is NOT correct.

Explanation:

Role of Arteries and Veins:

Arteries and veins move blood in our body.

Usually, arteries carry oxygen-rich (oxygenated) blood from the heart to the body. Veins bring back oxygen-poor (deoxygenated) blood from the body to the heart.

Exception in Pulmonary Circulation:

In the case of the lungs, this rule is opposite:

The pulmonary artery takes deoxygenated blood from the heart (right ventricle) to the lungs, so it can get oxygen.

The pulmonary vein brings oxygenated blood from the lungs back to the heart (left atrium).

So, here arteries carry deoxygenated blood and veins carry oxygenated blood, which is the opposite of the usual rule.

hi.content: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->मानव में धमनियों और शिराओं के बारे में निम्नलिखित में से कौन-सा कथन सही नहीं है ?

options.content: धमनियां हृदय से रक्त ले जाती हैं जबकि शिराएं हृदय की ओर रक्त लाती है
फुप्फुसी धमनियां सदैव ऑक्सीजनयुक्त रक्त ले जाती हैं जबकि फुफ्फुसी शिराएं सदैव ऑक्सीजनरहित रक्त ले जाती हैं।
सामान्यतः शिराओं की तुलना में धमनियों की दीवारें अधिक मोटी और लचीली होती हैं।
धमनियों में वाल्व नहीं होते हैं किंतु शिराओं में वाल्व होते हैं

hi.explanation:
"फुफ्फुसीय धमनी (Pulmonary arteries) हमेशा ऑक्सीजन युक्त रक्त ले जाती हैं जबकि फुफ्फुसीय शिरा (Pulmonary veins) हमेशा ऑक्सीजन रहित रक्त ले जाती हैं" कथन सही नहीं है।

व्याख्या:

धमनी और शिरा की भूमिका:

धमनी और शिरा हमारे शरीर में रक्त परिसंचरण का कार्य करती हैं।

सामान्यतः, धमनी (artery) हृदय से शरीर के विभिन्न भागों तक ऑक्सीजन युक्त (ऑक्सीजनित) रक्त पहुंचाती हैं। शिराएं (vein) शरीर के विभिन्न भागों से ऑक्सीजन रहित (डिऑक्सीजनेटेड) रक्त हृदय की ओर लौटाती हैं।

फुफ्फुसीय परिसंचरण में अपवाद:

फेफड़ों के मामले में यह नियम उल्टा होता है:

फुफ्फुसीय धमनी (Pulmonary artery) हृदय के दाहिने निलय (right ventricle) से ऑक्सीजन रहित रक्त को फेफड़ों तक ले जाती है, जिससे रक्त ऑक्सीजन प्राप्त कर सके।

फुफ्फुसीय शिरा (Pulmonary vein) फेफड़ों से ऑक्सीजन युक्त रक्त हृदय के बाएं आलिंद (left atrium) में वापस लाती है।

इस प्रकार, यहाँ धमनी डिऑक्सीजनेटेड रक्त ले जाती है और शिरा ऑक्सीजनित रक्त लाती है, जो सामान्य नियम के विपरीत है।

options: Involuntary actions such as blood pressure, salivation vomiting ete.
Controlling the regions receiving sensory impulses from receptors
Controlling the movement of voluntary muscles
Sensation of hunger or being full
question: Identify the functions that are controlled by the hind brain of human being

en.content: Identify the functions that are controlled by the hind brain of human being

options.content: Involuntary actions such as blood pressure, salivation vomiting ete.
Controlling the regions receiving sensory impulses from receptors
Controlling the movement of voluntary muscles
Sensation of hunger or being full

en.explanation:
The correct answer is Option 1: Involuntary actions such as blood pressure, salivation vomiting etc.

Let us understand this step-by-step by looking at the divisions of the human brain as described in your NCERT textbook:

  1. The human brain is divided into three major parts or regions: the fore-brain, mid-brain, and hind-brain.

  2. The hind-brain plays a major role in controlling involuntary actions (actions that happen without our conscious thought). It consists of three parts: the pons, medulla, and cerebellum.

According to the NCERT textbook, involuntary actions such as blood pressure, salivation, and vomiting are strictly controlled by the medulla present in the hind-brain. Therefore, Option 1 is correct.

Let us also look at the other options to understand why they are incorrect:

  • Option 2 (Sensory impulses): The fore-brain is the main thinking part of the brain. It possesses specialized regions that receive sensory impulses from various receptors for hearing, smell, sight, etc.

  • Option 3 (Voluntary muscles): The fore-brain contains motor areas that control the movement of voluntary muscles (like your leg muscles when you decide to walk). While a part of the hind-brain (the cerebellum) helps in the precision and balance of these movements, the actual control and decision of voluntary movement come from the fore-brain.

  • Option 4 (Sensation of hunger): The sensation of feeling hungry or being full is controlled by a separate, specific centre associated with the fore-brain.

Thus, comparing all the functions, Option 1 is the correct function controlled by the hind-brain.

hi.content: उन प्रकार्यों की पहचान कीजिए जो मनुष्य के पश्च मस्तिष्क द्वारा नियंत्रित किए जाते हैं ?

options.content: अनैच्छिक क्रियाएं जैसे रक्तदाब, लार आना, वमन आदि
ग्राही अंगों से संवेदी आवेग प्राप्त करने वाले क्षेत्रों का नियंत्रण
स्वैच्छिक मांसपेशियों की गति को नियंत्रित करना
भूख या पेट भरा होने की संवेदना

hi.explanation:
सही उत्तर है विकल्प 1: अनैच्छिक क्रियाएँ जैसे रक्तचाप, लार आना, उल्टी आदि।

आइए इसे चरण-दर-चरण समझते हैं और आपकी एनसीईआरटी पाठ्यपुस्तक में वर्णित मानव मस्तिष्क के विभाजनों को देखते हैं:

  1. मानव मस्तिष्क को तीन प्रमुख भागों या क्षेत्रों में बाँटा गया है: अग्र मस्तिष्क (fore-brain), मध्य मस्तिष्क (mid-brain), और पश्च मस्तिष्क (hind-brain)।

  2. पश्च मस्तिष्क (hind-brain) अनैच्छिक क्रियाओं (वे क्रियाएँ जो हमारी चेतना के बिना होती हैं) को नियंत्रित करने में मुख्य भूमिका निभाता है। इसमें तीन भाग होते हैं: पोंस, मेडुला और सेरिबेलम।

एनसीईआरटी पाठ्यपुस्तक के अनुसार, रक्तचाप, लार आना और उल्टी जैसी अनैच्छिक क्रियाओं का नियंत्रण मुख्य रूप से पश्च मस्तिष्क में उपस्थित मेडुला द्वारा किया जाता है। अतः विकल्प 1 सही है।

आइए अन्य विकल्पों को भी देखें कि वे क्यों गलत हैं:

  • विकल्प 2 (संवेदी आवेग): अग्र मस्तिष्क (fore-brain) मस्तिष्क का मुख्य सोचने वाला भाग है। इसमें विशेष क्षेत्र होते हैं, जो श्रवण, गंध, दृश्य आदि के विभिन्न अभिग्राहकों से संवेदी आवेग प्राप्त करते हैं।

  • विकल्प 3 (इच्छायुक्त पेशियाँ): अग्र मस्तिष्क (fore-brain) में वह क्षेत्र होता है जो इच्छायुक्त पेशियों (जैसे पैरों की मांसपेशियाँ जब आप चलने का निर्णय लेते हैं) की गति नियंत्रित करता है। यद्यपि पश्च मस्तिष्क का एक भाग (सेरिबेलम) इन गतियों की सटीकता और संतुलन में सहायता करता है, लेकिन असल नियंत्रण और इच्छित गति का निर्णय अग्र मस्तिष्क से आता है।

  • विकल्प 4 (भूख का अहसास): भूख लगना या तृप्ति का अनुभव होना अग्र मस्तिष्क से जुड़े विशेष केंद्र द्वारा नियंत्रित किया जाता है।

इस प्रकार, सभी कार्यों की तुलना करने पर, विकल्प 1 वह सही कार्य है जिसे पश्च मस्तिष्क नियंत्रित करता है।

solution:
The correct answer is Option 1: Involuntary actions such as blood pressure, salivation vomiting etc.

Let us understand this step-by-step by looking at the divisions of the human brain as described in your NCERT textbook:

  1. The human brain is divided into three major parts or regions: the fore-brain, mid-brain, and hind-brain.

  2. The hind-brain plays a major role in controlling involuntary actions (actions that happen without our conscious thought). It consists of three parts: the pons, medulla, and cerebellum.

According to the NCERT textbook, involuntary actions such as blood pressure, salivation, and vomiting are strictly controlled by the medulla present in the hind-brain. Therefore, Option 1 is correct.

Let us also look at the other options to understand why they are incorrect:

  • Option 2 (Sensory impulses): The fore-brain is the main thinking part of the brain. It possesses specialized regions that receive sensory impulses from various receptors for hearing, smell, sight, etc.

  • Option 3 (Voluntary muscles): The fore-brain contains motor areas that control the movement of voluntary muscles (like your leg muscles when you decide to walk). While a part of the hind-brain (the cerebellum) helps in the precision and balance of these movements, the actual control and decision of voluntary movement come from the fore-brain.

  • Option 4 (Sensation of hunger): The sensation of feeling hungry or being full is controlled by a separate, specific centre associated with the fore-brain.

Thus, comparing all the functions, Option 1 is the correct function controlled by the hind-brain.

correctAnswer: Involuntary actions such as blood pressure, salivation vomiting ete.

question: Iodized salt is a vital part of our diet as it is essential for the synthesis of

en.content: Iodized salt is a vital part of our diet as it is essential for the synthesis of
en.explanation:
The correct answer is Option 2: Thyroxin

Explanation:

  1. Iodine is a micronutrient required by our body in very small amounts.

  2. The thyroid gland, located in the neck, uses iodine to produce the hormone thyroxin.

  3. Thyroxin controls the metabolic rate of the body and helps in growth and development.

  4. A deficiency of iodine leads to goitre, a disease in which the thyroid gland enlarges.

Therefore, iodized salt is included in our diet to provide iodine for the synthesis of thyroxin.

question: The menstrual cycle in females is controlled by hormones that are secreted by

en.content: The menstrual cycle in females is controlled by hormones that are secreted by
en.explanation:
The correct option is:

Option 3: Ovary and pituitary gland

Explanation:

  1. The pituitary gland (present in the brain) secretes two important hormones:

  • Follicle Stimulating Hormone (FSH)

  • Luteinising Hormone (LH)

    These hormones act on the ovaries.

  1. The ovaries respond to these hormones and secrete:

  • Oestrogen

  • Progesterone

  1. Together, these hormones regulate different stages of the menstrual cycle — the maturation of the egg, release of the egg (ovulation), and preparation of the uterus for possible pregnancy.

Hence, the menstrual cycle is controlled by the ovary and pituitary gland.

question: Which one of the following blood cells type secrete large number of antibodies during am immune response of the body?

en.content: Which one of the following blood cells type secrete large number of antibodies during am immune response of the body?
en.explanation:
The correct answer is:

Option 3: Lymphocytes

Explanation:

During an immune response, B-lymphocytes get activated and change into plasma cells. These plasma cells secrete a large number of antibodies.

Why not the others?

  • Neutrophils: They mainly engulf and destroy microbes.

  • Eosinophils: They are involved in allergic reactions and defense against parasites.

  • Monocytes: They become macrophages and help in phagocytosis.

So, the blood cells that secrete large numbers of antibodies are lymphocytes.

question: Which of the following organelles have their own DNA and ribosomes ?

en.content: Which of the following organelles have their own DNA and ribosomes ?
en.explanation:
The correct answer is Option 2: Mitochondria and Chloroplast.

Explanation:

Mitochondria and chloroplast are called semi-autonomous organelles because they have:

  • their own DNA

  • their own ribosomes

This helps them to make some of their own proteins.

Why not the other options?

  • Endoplasmic reticulum and Golgi bodies do not have their own DNA or ribosomes.

  • Cytoskeleton does not have DNA or ribosomes.

  • Cell wall is a non-living covering, and nucleolus does not have its own DNA and ribosomes like mitochondria and chloroplast.

Thus, the answer is:

$$\boxed{\text{Mitochondria and Chloroplast}}$$

question: The lactic acid that gets accumulated in the muscle cells during rigorous exercise causing muscle cramps is produced from

en.content: The lactic acid that gets accumulated in the muscle cells during rigorous exercise causing muscle cramps is produced from
en.explanation:
The lactic acid formed in muscle under anaerobic (low-O₂) conditions comes from pyruvate. Specifically, lactate dehydrogenase catalyzes:

$$\text{pyruvate} + \text{NADH} + H^+ \;\longrightarrow\; \text{lactate} + \text{NAD}^+$$

So the correct choice is Option 2: Pyruvate.

hi.content: कठोर व्यायाम के दौरान मांसपेशियों की कोशिकाओं में संचित होने वाला लैक्टिक अम्ल, मांसपेशियों में ऐंठन का कारण बनता है, यह उत्पन्न होता है
hi.explanation:
मांसपेशियों में अलवायुजी (कम O₂) परिस्थितियों में बनने वाला लैक्टिक अम्ल पायुरुवेट से बनता है। विशेष रूप से, लैक्टेट डिहाइड्रोजनेज निम्न अभिक्रिया को उत्प्रेरित करता है:

$$\text{पायुरुवेट} + \text{NADH} + H^+ \;\longrightarrow\; \text{लैक्टेट} + \text{NAD}^+$$

इसलिए सही विकल्प है विकल्प 2: पायुरुवेट।

question: A chain of peptide containing linear sequences of amino acid linked by peptide bonds best represent the

en.content: A chain of peptide containing linear sequences of amino acid linked by peptide bonds best represent the
en.explanation:
The description “a linear sequence of amino acids linked by peptide bonds” refers to the protein’s

Primary structure

Briefly:

Primary structure is simply the order of amino acids in the polypeptide chain.

Secondary structure involves local folding (α-helices, β-sheets).

Tertiary structure is the overall 3D shape of a single chain.

Quaternary structure is the assembly of multiple chains.

question: Cambium is an example of

en.content: Cambium is an example of
en.explanation:
Cambium is a classic example of a lateral meristem—tissue that drives the thickening (secondary growth) of stems and roots.

Why it’s not the others:

Apical meristem (Option 2) occurs at shoot and root tips and is responsible for lengthwise growth.

Intercalary meristem (Option 3) is found at the base of nodes (common in grasses) and helps elongate internodes.

Permanent tissue (Option 4) consists of mature, non-dividing cells.

Answer: Option 1 – Lateral meristem.

hi.content: एधा इसका एक उदाहरण है
hi.explanation:
कैम्बियम एक पार्श्व मेरिस्टीम (lateral meristem) का उत्कृष्ट उदाहरण है—यह ऊतक तनों और जड़ों की मोटाई (द्वितीयक वृद्धि/secondary growth) में योगदान देता है।

अन्य विकल्प क्यों नहीं हैं:

शीर्षस्थ मेरिस्टीम (विकल्प 2) तने और जड़ के अग्रभाग पर पाया जाता है तथा यह लम्बाई में वृद्धि के लिए उत्तरदायी है।

अन्तर्वर्ती मेरिस्टीम (विकल्प 3) पर्व-संधि (nodes) के आधार पर पाया जाता है (घास में सामान्य), और यह अन्तरपर्व (internode) की लम्बाई बढ़ाने में सहायता करता है।

स्थायी ऊतक (विकल्प 4) में परिपक्व एवं विभाजन रहित (non-dividing) कोशिकाएँ होती हैं।

उत्तर: विकल्प 1 – पार्श्व मेरिस्टीम।

question: Which is the correct base pairing found in a normal DNA molecule?

en.content: Which is the correct base pairing found in a normal DNA molecule?
en.explanation:
The correct pairing in normal DNA is:

Adenine (A) pairs with Thymine (T)

Cytosine (C) pairs with Guanine (G)

So the answer is Option 1: Adenine pairs with Thymine.

question: Clotting of blood involves which one among the following clotting proteins?

en.content: Clotting of blood involves which one among the following clotting proteins?
en.explanation:
The correct answer is Option 2: Fibrinogen.

Explanation:

Fibrinogen (also called Factor I) is a soluble plasma protein.

During clotting, the enzyme thrombin cleaves fibrinogen to form insoluble fibrin strands.

These fibrin strands weave through the platelet plug, stabilizing it and forming the blood clot.

question: Initial discovery of antibiotic penicillin was done by

en.content: Initial discovery of antibiotic penicillin was done by
en.explanation:
The correct answer is Alexander Fleming.

Concept:

Antibiotics are compounds that either kill bacteria or inhibit their growth, significantly transforming medical treatment for bacterial infections.

The discovery of penicillin, the first widely used antibiotic, was a monumental breakthrough in medicine, saving countless lives.

Penicillin was discovered accidentally by Alexander Fleming in 1928. He observed that a mold named Penicillium notatum produced a substance that could inhibit bacterial growth.

Explanation of Correct Answer:

Alexander Fleming was a Scottish bacteriologist credited with discovering penicillin in 1928.

During his research, he noticed that colonies of the bacterium Staphylococcus aureus were destroyed by a mold contaminant on a culture plate.

The mold was identified as Penicillium notatum, and the substance it produced was named "penicillin." Fleming's discovery led to a Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine in 1945, shared with Howard Florey and Ernst Boris Chain.

Other Options:

Francis Crick: Crick was a British molecular biologist and neuroscientist known for co-discovering the structure of DNA with James Watson in 1953.

Maurice Wilkins: A British physicist and molecular biologist, Wilkins contributed to the discovery of DNA's double-helix structure, working closely with Rosalind Franklin. He shared the 1962 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine with Watson and Crick.

Charles Darwin: A British naturalist, Darwin is renowned for his theory of evolution by natural selection, detailed in his 1859 work, On the Origin of Species.

hi.content: प्रतिजैविक पेनिसिलिन की प्रारंभिक खोज किसने की थी
hi.explanation:
सही उत्तर है अलेक्जेंडर फ्लेमिंग।

अवधारणा:

एंटीबायोटिक्स ऐसे यौगिक होते हैं जो या तो जीवाणुओं (बैक्टीरिया) को मारते हैं या उनकी वृद्धि को रोकते हैं, जिसने जीवाणु संक्रमणों के उपचार में क्रांतिकारी परिवर्तन ला दिया।

पेनिसिलिन की खोज, जो कि प्रथम व्यापक रूप से उपयोग की जाने वाली एंटीबायोटिक है, चिकित्सा जगत में एक ऐतिहासिक उपलब्धि थी, जिससे अनगिनत लोगों की जान बची।

पेनिसिलिन की accidental (अकस्मात्) खोज अलेक्जेंडर फ्लेमिंग ने 1928 में की थी। उन्होंने देखा कि पेनिसिलियम नोटेटम नामक फफूंदी एक ऐसा पदार्थ उत्पन्न करती है, जो जीवाणुओं की वृद्धि को रोक सकती है।

सही उत्तर की व्याख्या:

अलेक्जेंडर फ्लेमिंग एक स्कॉटिश जीवाणु विज्ञानी (bacteriologist) थे, जिन्हें 1928 में पेनिसिलिन की खोज का श्रेय दिया जाता है।

अपने शोध के दौरान, उन्होंने पाया कि स्टैफिलोकोकस ऑरियस जीवाणु की कॉलोनियों को एक फफूंदी द्वारा नष्ट कर दिया गया था, जो प्रयोगशाला के कल्चर प्लेट पर मौजूद थी।

उस फफूंदी की पहचान पेनिसिलियम नोटेटम के रूप में की गई, और उसके द्वारा उत्पन्न पदार्थ को 'पेनिसिलिन' नाम दिया गया। फ्लेमिंग की खोज ने चिकित्सा या शरीर विज्ञान (Physiology or Medicine) के क्षेत्र में 1945 का नोबेल पुरस्कार दिलाया, जिसे उन्होंने हावर्ड फ्लोरी और अर्न्स्ट बोरिस चेन के साथ साझा किया।

अन्य विकल्प:

फ्रांसिस क्रिक: क्रिक एक ब्रिटिश आणविक जीवविज्ञानी और तंत्रिका विज्ञानी थे, जो जेम्स वाटसन के साथ मिलकर 1953 में डीएनए की संरचना की खोज के लिए प्रसिद्ध हैं।

मॉरिस विल्किन्स: एक ब्रिटिश भौतिक विज्ञानी एवं आणविक जीवविज्ञानी, विल्किन्स ने डीएनए की द्विगुनी सर्पिल (double-helix) संरचना की खोज में योगदान दिया। वे रोज़लिंड फ्रैंकलिन के साथ मिलकर काम करते थे और 1962 में वाटसन और क्रिक के साथ चिकित्सा या शरीर विज्ञान में नोबेल पुरस्कार साझा किया।

चार्ल्स डार्विन: एक ब्रिटिश प्रकृतिवादी, डार्विन अपने प्राकृतिक चयन (natural selection) के सिद्धांत के लिए प्रसिद्ध हैं, जिसे उन्होंने 1859 में प्रकाशित अपने ग्रंथ ऑन द ओरिजिन ऑफ स्पीशीज़ में विस्तारपूर्वक वर्णित किया।

en.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
en.subTitle: Biology • General Science

hi.excerpt: प्रतिजैविक पेनिसिलिन की प्रारंभिक खोज किसने की थी

solution:
The correct answer is Alexander Fleming.

Concept:

Antibiotics are compounds that either kill bacteria or inhibit their growth, significantly transforming medical treatment for bacterial infections.

The discovery of penicillin, the first widely used antibiotic, was a monumental breakthrough in medicine, saving countless lives.

Penicillin was discovered accidentally by Alexander Fleming in 1928. He observed that a mold named Penicillium notatum produced a substance that could inhibit bacterial growth.

Explanation of Correct Answer:

Alexander Fleming was a Scottish bacteriologist credited with discovering penicillin in 1928.

During his research, he noticed that colonies of the bacterium Staphylococcus aureus were destroyed by a mold contaminant on a culture plate.

The mold was identified as Penicillium notatum, and the substance it produced was named "penicillin." Fleming's discovery led to a Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine in 1945, shared with Howard Florey and Ernst Boris Chain.

Other Options:

Francis Crick: Crick was a British molecular biologist and neuroscientist known for co-discovering the structure of DNA with James Watson in 1953.

Maurice Wilkins: A British physicist and molecular biologist, Wilkins contributed to the discovery of DNA's double-helix structure, working closely with Rosalind Franklin. He shared the 1962 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine with Watson and Crick.

Charles Darwin: A British naturalist, Darwin is renowned for his theory of evolution by natural selection, detailed in his 1859 work, On the Origin of Species.

question: Which one of the following statements about lysosomes is NOT correct ?

en.content: Which one of the following statements about lysosomes is NOT correct ?

options.content: They are waste disposal system of the cell
They breakdown all inorganic materials
These are also referred to as “suicidal bags”
Lysosomes stop entry of foreign materials

en.explanation:
Option 2

  1. Lysosomes contain acid hydrolases that digest organic macromolecules (proteins, lipids, carbohydrates, nucleic acids), not inorganic substances.

  2. They act as the “waste-disposal system” by breaking down cellular debris and pathogens.

  3. Because they can digest the cell’s own components in case of damage, they are called “suicidal bags.”

  4. Prevention of entry of foreign materials is a function of the cell membrane (e.g., receptor-mediated endocytosis), not of lysosomes.

question: Which of the following two cell organelles have their own genetic material ?

en.content: Which of the following two cell organelles have their own genetic material ?
en.explanation:
1. Organelles with their own genetic material are those that contain DNA independent of the nuclear genome.

  2. Mitochondria

    • Contain a small circular DNA (mtDNA) in their matrix.

    • Encode some proteins needed for oxidative phosphorylation.

  3. Plastids (e.g., chloroplasts)

    • Contain their own circular DNA (cpDNA) in the stroma.

    • Encode proteins for photosynthesis and other functions.

  4. Endoplasmic reticulum, lysosomes and Golgi apparatus do not contain DNA.

Answer: Option 3 (Mitochondria and plastids)

question: Which one among the following tissues help to connect two bones ?

en.content: Which one among the following tissues help to connect two bones ?
en.explanation:
The correct answer is Option 2: Ligaments.

Here's why:

  • Ligaments are a type of dense connective tissue that are elastic and have considerable strength. Their primary function is to connect two bones together at a joint. They contain very little matrix.

  • Tendons are also a type of dense connective tissue, but they connect muscles to bones. They are strong and inelastic.

  • Areolar tissue is a loose connective tissue found between the skin and muscles, around blood vessels and nerves, and in the bone marrow. It fills the space inside the organs and helps in tissue repair. It does not primarily connect bones.

  • Cartilage is another type of connective tissue with a solid but flexible matrix. It is found in the nose tip, outer ear, trachea, and larynx. It also smoothens bone surfaces at joints but does not directly connect two bones.

question: Which one among the following is the source of energy in cells ?

en.content: Which one among the following is the source of energy in cells ?
en.explanation:
1. ATP stands for Adenosine Triphosphate.

  2. Structure of ATP:

    • Adenine (nitrogenous base)

    • Ribose (5-carbon sugar)

    • Three phosphate groups (–P–P–P)

  3. Hydrolysis reaction:

    $$\mathrm{ATP + H_2O \longrightarrow ADP + P_i + \text{Energy}}$$

    • $P_i$ = inorganic phosphate

    • Energy is released when the terminal (third) phosphate bond breaks

  4. Role of ATP in cells:

    • Provides energy for all cellular activities (muscle contraction, active transport, biosynthesis)

    • Acts as a rechargeable “energy currency”:

    – ADP + $P_i$ can be converted back into ATP during cellular respiration

  5. Comparison with other nucleotides:

    • ADP (Adenosine Diphosphate): has only two phosphates – lower energy

    • AMP (Adenosine Monophosphate): one phosphate – still lower energy

    • NAD (Nicotinamide Adenine Dinucleotide): coenzyme for redox reactions, not a direct energy currency

Answer: Option 2, ATP.

question: In which one among the following the vegetative propagation takes place through Eye Buds ?

en.content: In which one among the following the vegetative propagation takes place through Eye Buds ?
en.explanation:
1. Vegetative propagation: asexual reproduction in plants without seeds, producing clones of the parent.

  2. Main methods (NCERT):

    a. Stem tubers – potato

    b. Bulbs – onion

    c. Rhizomes – ginger

    d. Stem cuttings/setts – sugarcane

  3. Eye buds (“eyes”) are small vegetative buds on stem tubers.

  4. Potato tubers bear these eyes; each eye can give rise to a new shoot and then a whole plant.

Answer: Option 1 Potato.

question: Which one among the following separation techniques is used for blood tests in diagnostic laboratories ?

en.content: Which one among the following separation techniques is used for blood tests in diagnostic laboratories ?
en.explanation:
The correct answer is:

Option 3 — Centrifugation

Explanation:

  1. In diagnostic laboratories, blood samples are collected to test plasma, serum, or different components.

  2. Since blood is a mixture of plasma, red blood cells, white blood cells, and platelets, we need to separate these components.

  3. Centrifugation is the process where the sample is rotated at very high speed in a machine called a centrifuge.

  4. Due to centrifugal force, the denser particles (like red blood cells) settle at the bottom, and the lighter components (like plasma) stay at the top.

  5. This allows doctors to test specific parts of blood easily.

Hence, centrifugation is the technique used for blood tests in diagnostic laboratories.

  3. Centrifugation is the process where the sample is rotated at very high speed in a machine called a centrifuge.

  4. Due to centrifugal force, the denser particles (like red blood cells) settle at the bottom, and the lighter components (like plasma) stay at the top.

  5. This allows doctors to test specific parts of blood easily.

Hence, centrifugation is the technique used for blood tests in diagnostic laboratories.

question: Which one among the following diseases is caused due to deficiency of Vitamin D in humans?

en.content: Which one among the following diseases is caused due to deficiency of Vitamin D in humans?
en.explanation:
The correct answer is Rickets.

Concept:

Vitamins are essential nutrients required for the body to function properly. A lack of certain vitamins can lead to specific health conditions or diseases.

Vitamin D is especially important for maintaining healthy bones and teeth.

It aids in the absorption of calcium and phosphorus, which are critical for forming bone structure.

A deficiency in Vitamin D can result in rickets in children, a condition that negatively impacts bone development, potentially leading to bone pain, delayed growth, and skeletal deformities.

Explanation:

Rickets: Caused by a deficiency of Vitamin D, resulting in weakened bones, bone pain, and skeletal deformities in children. Severe cases can lead to growth issues and fractures.

Beriberi: Due to a deficiency of Vitamin B1 (thiamine), affecting cardiovascular and nervous systems, with symptoms like weakness, limb pain, and swelling.

Scurvy: Results from a lack of Vitamin C, causing bleeding gums, bruising, joint pain, and anemia.

Pneumonia: An infection that inflames the air sacs in one or both lungs, caused by bacteria, viruses, or fungi, not by a vitamin deficiency.

hi.content: ​निम्नलिखित में से कौन-सा रोग मनुष्यों में विटामिन D की कमी के कारण होता है?
hi.explanation:
सही उत्तर है रिकेट्स।

अवधारणा:

विटामिन ऐसे आवश्यक पोषक तत्व हैं जो शरीर के सही कार्य के लिए आवश्यक होते हैं। कुछ विटामिनों की कमी से विशेष स्वास्थ्य समस्याएँ या रोग हो सकते हैं।

विटामिन D स्वस्थ हड्डियों और दाँतों को बनाए रखने के लिए विशेष रूप से महत्वपूर्ण है।

यह कैल्शियम और फास्फोरस के अवशोषण में सहायक होता है, जो हड्डियों की संरचना के निर्माण के लिए अत्यंत आवश्यक हैं।

विटामिन D की कमी से बच्चों में रिकेट्स हो सकता है, यह एक ऐसी स्थिति है जिससे हड्डियों का विकास प्रभावित होता है, जिससे हड्डियों में दर्द, वृद्धि में देरी और कंकाल संबंधी विकृतियाँ हो सकती हैं।

व्याख्या:

रिकेट्स: विटामिन D की कमी से होता है, जिससे बच्चों में हड्डियाँ कमजोर हो जाती हैं, हड्डियों में दर्द और कंकाल में विकृति आ सकती है। गंभीर मामलों में वृद्धि में बाधा और हड्डियों में टूट-फूट की समस्या हो सकती है।

बेरी-बेरी: विटामिन B1 (थायमिन) की कमी के कारण होता है, जिससे हृदय एवं तंत्रिका तंत्र प्रभावित होते हैं, और कमजोरी, अंगों में दर्द तथा सूजन जैसे लक्षण प्रकट हो सकते हैं।

स्कर्वी: विटामिन C की कमी के कारण होता है, जिससे मसूड़ों से खून आना, शरीर पर नीले निशान, जोड़ों में दर्द और रक्ताल्पता (एनीमिया) जैसी समस्याएँ उत्पन्न हो सकती हैं।

निमोनिया: यह एक संक्रमण है जो एक या दोनों फेफड़ों के वायुसंचरण थैलों (एयर सैक्स) में सूजन उत्पन्न करता है। यह जीवाणु, विषाणु या फफूंदी के कारण होता है, विटामिन की कमी के कारण नहीं।

question: Which one among the following combinations most appropriately represent the components of balanced diet?

en.content: Which one among the following combinations most appropriately represent the components of balanced diet?

options.content: Carbohydrate, vitamins, proteins, fat, minerals, fibres and water.
Carbohydrate, vitamins, proteins, fat and minerals
<!-- x-tinymce/html -->Carbohydrate, vitamins, proteins, minerals and water
Vitamins, proteins, fat, minerals, fibres and water

en.explanation:
A balanced diet provides the necessary nutrients and energy for the body to function correctly. It includes a variety of foods in adequate proportions to ensure the intake of essential nutrients. The key components of a balanced diet include carbohydrates, proteins, fats, vitamins, minerals, fibers, and water:

Carbohydrates: They are the primary source of energy for the body. Foods like bread, rice, and pasta are rich in carbohydrates.

Proteins: Essential for growth, repair, and maintenance of body tissues. Sources include meat, fish, eggs, and legumes.

Fats: Necessary for energy storage, insulation, and protection of vital organs. Healthy sources include nuts, seeds, and oils.

Vitamins: They support various bodily functions, including immunity, energy production, and bone health. Found in fruits, vegetables, and dairy products.

Minerals: Essential for bone health, fluid balance, and muscle function. Common sources include leafy greens, nuts, seeds, and dairy.

Fibers: Important for digestive health and preventing constipation. They are found in whole grains, fruits, and vegetables.

Water: Crucial for hydration, temperature regulation, and transport of nutrients. Drinking sufficient water daily is vital.

hi.content: निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा संयोजन संतुलित आहार के घटकों का सबसे उपयुक्त प्रतिनिधित्व करता है?

options.content: कार्बोहाइड्रेट, विटामिन, प्रोटीन, वसा, खनिज, रेशा और जल
कार्बोहाइड्रेट, विटामिन, प्रोटीन, वसा और खनिज
<!-- x-tinymce/html -->कार्बोहाइड्रेट, विटामिन, प्रोटीन, खनिज और जल

hi.explanation:
संतुलित आहार शरीर के सही ढंग से कार्य करने के लिए आवश्यक पोषक तत्वों और ऊर्जा प्रदान करता है। इसमें आवश्यक पोषक तत्वों के सेवन को सुनिश्चित करने के लिए विभिन्न प्रकार के खाद्य पदार्थ पर्याप्त मात्रा में शामिल होते हैं। संतुलित आहार के मुख्य घटकों में कार्बोहाइड्रेट, प्रोटीन, वसा, विटामिन, खनिज, रेशा (फाइबर) और जल शामिल हैं:

कार्बोहाइड्रेट: ये शरीर के लिए ऊर्जा का मुख्य स्रोत हैं। रोटी, चावल और पास्ता जैसे खाद्य पदार्थ कार्बोहाइड्रेट से भरपूर होते हैं।

प्रोटीन: शरीर के ऊतकों की वृद्धि, मरम्मत और रख-रखाव के लिए आवश्यक हैं। मांस, मछली, अंडा और दालों में प्रोटीन पाया जाता है।

वसा: ऊर्जा संचयन, ऊष्मा संरक्षण तथा महत्त्वपूर्ण अंगों की सुरक्षा के लिए आवश्यक हैं। वसा के स्वस्थ स्रोतों में मेवे, बीज और तेल शामिल हैं।

विटामिन: ये शरीर की विभिन्न क्रियाओं जैसे प्रतिरक्षा, ऊर्जा उत्पादन और हड्डियों के स्वास्थ्य का समर्थन करते हैं। विटामिन फल, सब्ज़ियाँ और दुग्ध उत्पादों में पाए जाते हैं।

खनिज: हड्डियों के स्वास्थ्य, द्रव संतुलन तथा मांसपेशी की कार्य क्षमता के लिए आवश्यक हैं। इनके सामान्य स्रोत हरी पत्तेदार सब्ज़ियाँ, मेवे, बीज और दुग्ध उत्पाद हैं।

रेशा (फाइबर): पाचन तंत्र के स्वास्थ्य और कब्ज़ से बचाव के लिए महत्त्वपूर्ण है। रेशा साबुत अनाज, फल और सब्ज़ियों में पाया जाता है।

जल: यह शरीर में जल संतुलन, तापमान नियंत्रण और पोषक तत्वों के परिवहन के लिए आवश्यक है। प्रतिदिन पर्याप्त मात्रा में पानी पीना अत्यंत आवश्यक है।

question: In which part or organ of the human body is cartilage NOT found?

en.content: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->In which part or organ of the human body is cartilage NOT found?
en.explanation:
Cartilage is found in

• the nose (nasal cartilage)

• the trachea (C-shaped rings)

• the knee joint (articular cartilage)

Bronchioles, however, are small airways that lack cartilage and rely on smooth muscle to stay open.

Answer: Option 3 (Bronchiole)

hi.content: मानव शरीर के किस भाग या अंग में उपास्थि नहीं पाई जाती है?
hi.explanation:
उपास्थि पाई जाती है

• नाक में (नाक की उपास्थि)

• श्वास नली में (C-आकार के वलय)

• घुटने के जोड़ में (संयुक्त उपास्थि)

हालाँकि, ब्रोंकियोल्स छोटी वायुमार्ग होते हैं, जिनमें उपास्थि अनुपस्थित होती है और इन्हें खुला रखने के लिए चिकनी पेशियों पर निर्भर रहना पड़ता है।

उत्तर: विकल्प 3 (ब्रोंकियोल)

question: Which part of nephron of the human kidney is responsible for filtration of blood for excretion?

en.content: Which part of nephron of the human kidney is responsible for filtration of blood for excretion?
en.explanation:
1. Nephron (functional unit of kidney) consists of two main parts:

    a. Renal corpuscle (glomerulus + Bowman's capsule)

    b. Renal tubule (proximal convoluted tubule, loop of Henle, distal convoluted tubule, collecting duct)

  2. Filtration of blood takes place in the renal corpuscle:

    – Glomerulus: a network of capillaries where blood pressure forces water and small solutes out of blood.

    – Bowman's capsule: a cup-shaped structure that surrounds the glomerulus and collects the filtrate.

  3. Among the given options, the structure directly responsible for collecting the filtrate is

    Option 4: Bowman's capsule.

question: Organisms of which of the following phylum are called sponges?

en.content: Organisms of which of the following phylum are called sponges?
en.explanation:
Explanation:

Organisms belonging to the phylum Porifera are commonly known as sponges.

Porifera are simple, multicellular animals, primarily marine, though some species inhabit freshwater.

Sponges have porous bodies, a characteristic that facilitates water circulation, aiding in nutrient and oxygen intake and waste removal.

They possess a water transport or canal system.

Choanocytes, or collar cells, line the spongocoel and canals.

Digestion is intracellular.

The body's support structure is made of spicules or spongin fibers.

Sponges are hermaphrodites, producing both eggs and sperm within the same individual.

They reproduce asexually by fragmentation and sexually through gamete formation.

Fertilization is internal, and development is indirect, featuring a larval stage that is distinct from the adult form.

Examples include Sycon, Spongilla, and Euspongia.

Other Options:

Coelenterata (Cnidaria): Includes organisms like jellyfish, corals, and sea anemones. They have one body cavity and specialized cells (cnidocytes) with stinging organelles.

Platyhelminthes: Comprises flatworms, such as tapeworms and planarians. These bilaterally symmetrical organisms lack a body cavity (acoelomates) and often have parasitic members.

Echinodermata: Encompasses starfish, sea urchins, and sea cucumbers. They feature a unique water vascular system and adult bodies with five-part radial symmetry.

hi.content: निम्नलिखित में से किस संघ के जीवों को स्पंज कहा जाता है?
hi.explanation:
व्याख्या:

फाइलम पोरीफेरा के जीवों को सामान्यतः स्पंज (स्पॉन्ज) कहा जाता है।

पोरीफेरा साधारण, बहुकोशिकीय जन्तु होते हैं, जो मुख्यतः समुद्री होते हैं, हालांकि कुछ जातियाँ मीठे जल में भी पाई जाती हैं।

स्पंजो का शरीर झरझरा (छिद्रगर्भी) होता है, जो जल परिसंचरण में सहायक होता है, जिससे पोषक तत्वों और ऑक्सीजन का ग्रहण तथा अपशिष्ट पदार्थों का निष्कासन संभव होता है।

इनमें जल परिवहन या नलिका तंत्र (canal system) पाया जाता है।

कोएनोसाइट (collar cells), या कॉलर कोशिकाएँ, स्पॉन्जोकेल और नलिकाओं की अस्तरिका बनाती हैं।

पाचन अंतःकोशिकीय (intracellular) होता है।

इनका शारीरिक समर्थन या ढांचा स्पिक्यूल्स या स्पॉन्जिन तंतुओं से बना होता है।

स्पंज उभयलिंगी (hermaphrodite) होते हैं, अर्थात् एक ही जीव में अण्डाणु और शुक्राणु दोनों का निर्माण होता है।

वे अलैंगिक रीति से खंडित होकर (fragmentation) तथा लैंगिक रीति से युग्मक निर्माण द्वारा प्रजनन करते हैं।

निषेचन (fertilization) आंतरिक (internal) होता है तथा इनके जीवन चक्र में अप्रत्यक्ष विकास (indirect development) होता है, जिसमें लार्वा अवस्था वयस्क से भिन्न होती है।

इसके उदाहरण हैं - साइकेन (Sycon), स्पॉन्जिला (Spongilla) और यूस्पोंजिया (Euspongia)।

अन्य विकल्प:

कोएलेंटराटा (निडेरिया): इसमें जेलीफ़िश, मूंगे (corals) और समुद्री एनीमोनी (sea anemone) जैसे जीव आते हैं। इनमें एक ही शारीरिक गुहा (body cavity) होती है तथा विशेष कोशिकाएँ (निडोसाइट्स) पाई जाती हैं, जिनमें डंकने वाले अंगक (stinging organelles) होते हैं।

प्लैटीहेलमिंथेस: इसमें टेपवर्म (tapeworms) और प्लानैरिया (planarians) जैसे फ्लैटवर्म्स आते हैं। ये द्विपार्श्वीय सममित (bilaterally symmetrical) जीव होते हैं, जिनमें शरीर गुहा (body cavity) नहीं होती (एसीलॉमेट्स) तथा इनमें प्रायः परजीवी सदस्य होते हैं।

एकाइनोडर्मेटा: इसमें समुद्री तारा (starfish), समुद्री साही (sea urchins) और समुद्री ककड़ी (sea cucumbers) आते हैं। इनमें विशिष्ट जल संवहनी तंत्र (water vascular system) और वयस्क अवस्था में पंचखंडीय रेडियल सममिति (five-part radial symmetry) पाई जाती है।

en.excerpt: Organisms of which of the following phylum are called sponges?

hi.title: NDA General Ability 1st September 2024
hi.subTitle: Biology • General Science
hi.excerpt: निम्नलिखित में से किस संघ के जीवों को स्पंज कहा जाता है?

solution:
Explanation:

Organisms belonging to the phylum Porifera are commonly known as sponges.

Porifera are simple, multicellular animals, primarily marine, though some species inhabit freshwater.

Sponges have porous bodies, a characteristic that facilitates water circulation, aiding in nutrient and oxygen intake and waste removal.

They possess a water transport or canal system.

Choanocytes, or collar cells, line the spongocoel and canals.

Digestion is intracellular.

The body's support structure is made of spicules or spongin fibers.

Sponges are hermaphrodites, producing both eggs and sperm within the same individual.

They reproduce asexually by fragmentation and sexually through gamete formation.

Fertilization is internal, and development is indirect, featuring a larval stage that is distinct from the adult form.

Examples include Sycon, Spongilla, and Euspongia.

Other Options:

Coelenterata (Cnidaria): Includes organisms like jellyfish, corals, and sea anemones. They have one body cavity and specialized cells (cnidocytes) with stinging organelles.

Platyhelminthes: Comprises flatworms, such as tapeworms and planarians. These bilaterally symmetrical organisms lack a body cavity (acoelomates) and often have parasitic members.

Echinodermata: Encompasses starfish, sea urchins, and sea cucumbers. They feature a unique water vascular system and adult bodies with five-part radial symmetry.

options: They are composed of phospholipids and proteins only.
They are composed of phospholipids, proteins and cholesterol (lipid).
question: Which of the following statements regarding animal cell membrane is correct?

en.content: Which of the following statements regarding animal cell membrane is correct?

options.content: They are composed of phospholipids and proteins only.
They are composed of phospholipids, proteins and cholesterol (lipid).

en.explanation:
The correct answer is Option D: They are composed of phospholipids, proteins, and cholesterol (lipid).

The animal cell membrane, also known as the plasma membrane, is primarily composed of a lipid bilayer. This bilayer is made up of phospholipids, which are amphipathic molecules containing both hydrophilic (water-attracting) heads and hydrophobic (water-repelling) tails. This arrangement allows the formation of a semi-permeable membrane that serves as a barrier to the free passage of substances in and out of the cell.

In addition to phospholipids, the cell membrane includes proteins that are embedded within or attached to the lipid bilayer. These proteins serve a variety of functions, including transport, signal transduction, and acting as enzymes. The proteins are divided into two main categories: integral (or intrinsic) proteins, which span the entire membrane, and peripheral (or extrinsic) proteins, which are attached to the exterior or interior surfaces of the membrane.

Cholesterol is another crucial component of the animal cell membrane. It is interspersed among the phospholipids and plays a significant role in modifying the fluidity and stability of the membrane. Cholesterol molecules help to make the membrane less permeable to very small, water-soluble molecules that might otherwise freely cross it, and they also play a role in fluidity by preventing the fatty acid chains of the phospholipids from packing too closely together in low temperatures.

Mathematically, we can express the composition of the cell membrane as a mixture of lipids (including phospholipids and cholesterol) and proteins. This combination allows us to create the fluid mosaic model, which describes the membrane as a dynamic and fluid structure with various proteins embedded in or attached to a bilayer of phospholipids.

Overall, the cell membrane's structural complexity enables it to perform a wide range of biological functions essential for the survival and functionality of the cell.

question: Which of the following is not a part of compound microscope?

en.content: Which of the following is not a part of compound microscope?
en.explanation:
A compound microscope is a sophisticated device used to magnify small objects using multiple lenses. The key components of a compound microscope include the eyepiece (ocular lens), objective lenses, stage, mirror or light source, clips to hold the slide, and adjustment knobs among others.

Let's break down the options provided:

Option A: Mirror
A mirror is indeed a part of many compound microscopes, used to reflect light up through the specimen for clearer viewing.

Option B: Stage
The stage is a critical part of the compound microscope. It is the platform where the specimen slide is placed for observation.

Option C: Clip
Clips are also part of a compound microscope. They are used to hold the slide in place on the stage.

Option D: Retina
The retina is not a part of the compound microscope. The retina is actually a part of the human eye, not the microscope. It is the light-sensitive layer at the back of the eye that helps in vision.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option D: Retina

question: Which one of the following is the correct sequence in increasing complexity?

en.content: Which one of the following is the correct sequence in increasing complexity?
en.explanation:
The correct sequence in increasing complexity is Option D : Protein - Tissue - Organ - Organism.

Let's break down each component to understand the progression of complexity :

Protein : Proteins are complex molecules formed by chains of amino acids. They are fundamental units that carry out various functions within cells, including enzymatic, structural, and signaling roles.

Tissue : Tissues are groups of cells that work together to perform a specific function. There are several types of tissues, such as muscle tissue, nervous tissue, and epithelial tissue.

Organ : Organs are structures made up of different types of tissues that work together to perform specific functions. Examples include the heart, lungs, and liver.

Organism : An organism is a complete living entity capable of independent life. It is composed of various organ systems that function collectively to sustain life. Organisms can be as simple as a single-celled bacterium or as complex as a multicellular human being.

Hence, the hierarchy in increasing complexity is correctly represented by Option D : Protein - Tissue - Organ - Organism.

question:
Consider the following cell types :

1. Monocyte

2. Chondrocyte

3. Basophil

4. Lymphocyte

How many of the above belong to animal cell types?

en.content:
Consider the following cell types :

1. Monocyte

2. Chondrocyte

3. Basophil

4. Lymphocyte

How many of the above belong to animal cell types?
en.explanation:
All of the listed cell types—monocyte, chondrocyte, basophil, and lymphocyte—are animal cell types. To elaborate :

1. Monocyte : A type of white blood cell that is part of the immune system. It can differentiate into macrophages and dendritic cells which are crucial for immune response.

2. Chondrocyte : These are the only cells found in healthy cartilage. They produce and maintain the cartilaginous matrix, which is an important part of the skeletal system.

3. Basophil : Another type of white blood cell. Basophils are involved in inflammatory reactions and produce histamine and heparin during allergic responses.

4. Lymphocyte : These are a subtype of white blood cells found in the lymphatic system. They play a vital role in the immune system, with types including B cells, T cells, and natural killer cells.

Since all four types are indeed animal cell types, the correct answer is :

Option D

question: In the human digestive system, which one among the following is the role of the pancreas?

en.content: In the human digestive system, which one among the following is the role of the pancreas?

options.content: Secretion of surfactants to break up lipid droplets

en.explanation:
The correct answer is:

Option C

Secretion of lipase, amylase, and protease

The pancreas plays a crucial role in the human digestive system by secreting a variety of digestive enzymes. These enzymes include:

Lipase: This enzyme is responsible for breaking down fats into fatty acids and glycerol, aiding in the digestion and absorption of dietary fats.

Amylase: This enzyme helps in the digestion of carbohydrates by breaking down starches into sugars such as maltose and glucose.

Protease: These enzymes, including trypsin and chymotrypsin, break down proteins into smaller peptides and amino acids, which can then be absorbed by the body.

Collectively, these enzymes are essential for the proper digestion and absorption of nutrients from the food we eat. The pancreas secretes these enzymes into the small intestine, where they play a vital role in the digestive process.

question:
Consider the following statements :

1. DNA replication takes place when chromatin is opened up.

2. Chromatin organises itself into rod-shaped chromosomes before cell division.

3. Both prokaryotes and eukaryotes have the same process for cell division.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

en.content:
Consider the following statements :

1. DNA replication takes place when chromatin is opened up.

2. Chromatin organises itself into rod-shaped chromosomes before cell division.

3. Both prokaryotes and eukaryotes have the same process for cell division.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
en.explanation:
Let's analyze each statement one by one:

Statement 1: DNA replication takes place when chromatin is opened up.

DNA replication indeed occurs when the chromatin is in a more relaxed and open state. This allows the replication machinery to access the DNA strands and perform the copying process. Hence, statement 1 is correct.

Statement 2: Chromatin organises itself into rod-shaped chromosomes before cell division.

Before cell division, particularly during mitosis or meiosis, chromatin condenses and forms the distinct rod-shaped structures known as chromosomes. This condensation is crucial for the proper segregation of chromosomes into daughter cells. Hence, statement 2 is also correct.

Statement 3: Both prokaryotes and eukaryotes have the same process for cell division.

This statement is incorrect. Prokaryotes divide by a process called binary fission, which is simpler and involves the duplication and segregation of a single, circular DNA molecule. Eukaryotes, on the other hand, undergo more complex processes such as mitosis or meiosis, involving multiple linear chromosomes and various stages (prophase, metaphase, anaphase, telophase). Therefore, statement 3 is not correct.

Considering the analysis above, the correct answer is:

Option B - 1 and 2 only

question: Human body works in the pH range of :

en.content: Human body works in the pH range of :
en.explanation:
The human body typically functions optimally within a very narrow pH range, which is crucial for maintaining homeostasis and proper physiological functions. The ideal pH range for the human body is approximately 7.35 to 7.45. This slightly alkaline range is necessary for the proper function of cellular processes, enzyme activity, and overall metabolic functions.

Given the options provided:

Option A: 6.8 - 7.2

Option B: 7.0 - 7.8

Option C: 6.5 - 7.5

Option D: 7.5 - 8.0

The closest option to the actual pH range of 7.35 to 7.45 is Option B (7.0 - 7.8). While it is not a perfect match, it encompasses the ideal range better than the other options. Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option B: 7.0 - 7.8

question: Leaves of most plants appear green because the chlorophyll present in it :

en.content: Leaves of most plants appear green because the chlorophyll present in it :

options.content: absorbs red and blue light while reflecting green light.
absorbs green light while reflecting red and blue light.
reflects red light and absorbs blue and green light.

en.explanation:
Leaves of most plants appear green because the chlorophyll present in them predominantly absorbs light in the red and blue parts of the spectrum, while reflecting green light. This is why chlorophyll does not absorb much green light, and instead, it reflects it, making the leaves appear green to our eyes.

Therefore, the correct option is :

Option A: absorbs red and blue light while reflecting green light.

To explain this in more detail, we can consider the way light interacts with chlorophyll. Chlorophyll molecules have specific absorption spectra, meaning they absorb certain wavelengths of light more effectively. The absorption spectra of chlorophyll-a and chlorophyll-b are: $$ Chlorophyll-a: \text{peaks at approximately} \ 430 \ \text{nm (blue light) and} \ 662 \ \text{nm (red light)} $$ $$ Chlorophyll-b: \text{peaks at approximately} \ 453 \ \text{nm (blue light) and} \ 642 \ \text{nm (red light)} $$

The wavelengths of light that are not absorbed are reflected or transmitted, and for chlorophyll, the reflected light is mainly in the green part of the spectrum, around 500-550 nm.

This reflection of green light gives plants their characteristic green color.

question: In most prokaryotes, the chromosome number is:

en.content: In most prokaryotes, the chromosome number is:
en.explanation:
In most prokaryotes, the chromosome number is typically:

Option D

1

Prokaryotes, which include bacteria and archaea, generally have a single circular chromosome. This chromosome is not enclosed in a nucleus, unlike the linear chromosomes found in eukaryotic cells which are housed within a well-defined nucleus. The single circular chromosome of prokaryotes contains all the essential genetic information required for the cell's maintenance, metabolism, and reproduction.

Therefore, Option D (1) is the correct answer.

question: Bacterial DNA is referred to as naked because it is not associated with:

en.content: Bacterial DNA is referred to as naked because it is not associated with:
en.explanation:
Bacterial DNA is referred to as naked because it is not associated with proteins. In eukaryotic cells, DNA is wrapped around histone proteins forming a structure called nucleosome, which is a part of the chromatin. This association aids in the organization, compaction, and regulation of DNA. However, in prokaryotic cells like bacteria, the DNA does not have this histone association; hence, it is termed "naked." Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option B

proteins

question: Which one among the following cells produces antibodies against a foreign antigen?

en.content: Which one among the following cells produces antibodies against a foreign antigen?
en.explanation:
The correct option is Option A: Lymphocytes.

Lymphocytes are a type of white blood cell that play a crucial role in the body's immune response. They are primarily involved in the production of antibodies, which are proteins that specifically recognize and bind to foreign antigens, such as bacteria, viruses, and other pathogens. Once bound, antibodies can neutralize the pathogen directly or mark it for destruction by other immune cells.

More specifically, it is the B lymphocytes (or B cells) that are responsible for producing antibodies. When B cells encounter an antigen, they can differentiate into plasma cells, which are specialized in secreting large quantities of antibodies specific to that antigen.

Here's a brief explanation of the other options:

  • Erythrocytes: Also known as red blood cells, they primarily function to transport oxygen from the lungs to the tissues and carbon dioxide from the tissues to the lungs.
  • Eosinophils: These are another type of white blood cell which play a role in combating parasitic infections and are also involved in allergic responses.
  • Platelets: These are cell fragments that are crucial for blood clotting and wound healing. They help to stop bleeding by clumping and clotting blood vessel injuries.

Thus, the production of antibodies is a specific function of lymphocytes, particularly B cells, making option A the correct answer.

question: The green plants in a terrestrial eco-system absorbs sunlight that falls on their leaves and convert it into food energy. The percentage of solar radiation absorbed by all green plants for the process of photosynthesis is about

en.content: The green plants in a terrestrial eco-system absorbs sunlight that falls on their leaves and convert it into food energy. The percentage of solar radiation absorbed by all green plants for the process of photosynthesis is about
en.explanation:
The correct answer is Option A: 1%.

Here's why:

While sunlight is essential for photosynthesis, only a small portion of the total solar radiation that reaches the Earth's surface is actually absorbed and used by plants for this process.

Here's a breakdown:

  • A large portion of sunlight is reflected back into space by clouds, the atmosphere, and the Earth's surface itself.
  • Only a fraction of the remaining sunlight reaches the leaves of plants.
  • Of the light that reaches the leaves, only specific wavelengths are used for photosynthesis. Plants mainly absorb light in the blue and red regions of the visible spectrum.
  • Not all of the absorbed light is used for photosynthesis. Some is used for other processes within the plant, and some is lost as heat.

In summary, the percentage of solar radiation absorbed by green plants for photosynthesis is remarkably low. It's estimated to be around 1%, emphasizing the efficiency of the process and the importance of sunlight for life on Earth.

question: The two important features of sexual reproduction in higher organisms that create genetic diversity in offspring are

en.content: The two important features of sexual reproduction in higher organisms that create genetic diversity in offspring are
en.explanation:
In higher organisms, the two important features of sexual reproduction that create genetic diversity in offspring are meiosis and fertilization. Let’s explore why these two processes are crucial for genetic diversity.

Meiosis

Role in Genetic Diversity: Meiosis is a type of cell division that reduces the chromosome number by half, resulting in four daughter cells, each with a distinct set of chromosomes. This process introduces genetic variation through:

Crossing Over: During Prophase I of meiosis, homologous chromosomes pair up and exchange segments of genetic material. This recombination results in new combinations of genes.

Independent Assortment: During Metaphase I, the way in which different chromosome pairs align and separate is random. This leads to a variety of possible combinations of chromosomes in the gametes (sperm and egg cells).

Fertilization

Role in Genetic Diversity: Fertilization is the process where two gametes (sperm and egg) fuse to form a zygote. This fusion brings together genetic material from two distinct individuals, further contributing to the genetic diversity of the offspring. Since each parent contributes a unique set of alleles, the resulting zygote has a combination of traits different from either parent.

Conclusion

The correct option is Option B: Meiosis and fertilization. These processes are key to generating the genetic variability observed in offspring, which is a fundamental aspect of sexual reproduction in higher organisms.

Here is how we can summarize the options based on the key processes involved:

```html

Option Processes Role in Genetic Diversity
A Mitosis and fertilization Mitosis does not create genetic diversity; fertilization does.
B Meiosis and fertilization Both processes significantly contribute to genetic diversity.
C Mitosis and binary fission Neither process contributes to genetic diversity as they are forms of asexual reproduction.
D Meiosis and conjugation Meiosis contributes to genetic diversity; conjugation is not typical in higher organisms.

Therefore, Option B is the correct answer.

question: Eukaryotic cells are much more complex as compared to prokaryotes. Which one of the following structures is exclusively present in a eukaryotic cell?

en.content: Eukaryotic cells are much more complex as compared to prokaryotes. Which one of the following structures is exclusively present in a eukaryotic cell?
en.explanation:
Eukaryotic cells are indeed more complex than prokaryotic cells, possessing numerous structures that are absent in prokaryotes. To determine which structure from the options is exclusively present in a eukaryotic cell, let's analyze each one:

Option A: Cell wall

Cell walls are present in both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells. In prokaryotes, they are typically composed of peptidoglycan. In eukaryotes, plant cells have cell walls made of cellulose, and fungi have cell walls made of chitin. However, it is not exclusive to eukaryotic cells.

Option B: Plasma membrane

The plasma membrane, also known as the cell membrane, is a fundamental component of all cells, both prokaryotic and eukaryotic, responsible for protecting the cell from its environment and regulating the movement of substances in and out of the cell.

Option C: Nucleic acid

Nucleic acids, such as DNA and RNA, are found in both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells. They carry genetic information essential for cell function and reproduction.

Option D: Mitochondria

Mitochondria are exclusively found in eukaryotic cells. These organelles are known as the "powerhouses" of the cell, generating most of the cell's supply of adenosine triphosphate (ATP), which is used as a source of chemical energy. Prokaryotic cells do not have membrane-bound organelles such as mitochondria.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option D: Mitochondria

question: An important cause of tooth decay by the bacterium Streptococcus mutans is because of their ability to attach to teeth surface by producing slime layer from

en.content: An important cause of tooth decay by the bacterium Streptococcus mutans is because of their ability to attach to teeth surface by producing slime layer from
en.explanation:
Tooth decay, also known as dental caries, is primarily caused by the bacterium Streptococcus mutans. One of the critical factors in this process is the bacterium's ability to adhere to the surface of teeth. Streptococcus mutans does this by producing a sticky slime layer that allows it to form biofilms on the tooth surface. This slime layer is synthesized from dietary sugars.

When Streptococcus mutans metabolizes sugar, it produces lactic acid, which contributes to the demineralization of the tooth enamel. Additionally, the sticky slime layer composed of polysaccharides helps the bacteria to cling to the teeth and form plaque, which further exacerbates the process of tooth decay.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option A: Sugar

options: Both the gametes fuse with a single egg cell
Both the gametes fuse with two different egg cells
One gamete fuses with the egg cell and the other one eventually degenerate

question: In angiosperms, pollen grain germinates to produce two male gametes. Which one of the following functions is carried out by these gametes?

en.content: In angiosperms, pollen grain germinates to produce two male gametes. Which one of the following functions is carried out by these gametes?

options.content: Both the gametes fuse with a single egg cell
Both the gametes fuse with two different egg cells
One gamete fuses with the egg cell and the other one eventually degenerate
One gamete fuses with the egg cell and the other one fuses with a diploid secondary nucleus

en.explanation:
In angiosperms, the process of double fertilization takes place, which is a unique and defining feature of this group of plants. During this process, a pollen grain germinates on the stigma and forms a pollen tube, which extends down through the style to reach the ovule in the ovary. The generative cell within the pollen tube divides to form two male gametes (sperm cells).

Among these two male gametes, one fuses with the egg cell to form a diploid zygote, which will eventually develop into an embryo. The other male gamete fuses with the diploid secondary nucleus (which is formed by the fusion of the two polar nuclei in the central cell of the embryo sac) to form a triploid cell. This triploid cell develops into the endosperm, which will provide nourishment to the developing embryo.

Therefore, the correct function of these gametes is described by Option D. Here’s the explanation in a more summarized form:

Option D: One gamete fuses with the egg cell and the other one fuses with a diploid secondary nucleus.

In summary, double fertilization in angiosperms involves the following:

  1. One male gamete fuses with the egg cell to form the zygote (2n).

  1. The other male gamete fuses with the diploid secondary nucleus to form the triploid endosperm (3n).

This can be represented in the following manner using LaTeX:

$$ \text{First Fusion:} \quad Sperm (\text{n}) + Egg (\text{n}) \rightarrow Zygote (\text{2n}) $$

$$ \text{Second Fusion:} \quad Sperm (\text{n}) + Secondary Nucleus (\text{2n}) \rightarrow Endosperm (\text{3n}) $$

en.excerpt: In angiosperms, pollen grain germinates to produce two male gametes. Which one of the following functions is carried out by these gametes?

hi.title: NDA General Ability 3 September 2023
hi.subTitle: Biology • General Science
hi.excerpt: आवृतबीजियों में, परागकण के अंकुरण से दो नर युग्मक उत्पन्न होते हैं। इन युग्मकों द्वारा निम्नलिखित में से कौन-सा कार्य किया जाता है?

solution:
In angiosperms, the process of double fertilization takes place, which is a unique and defining feature of this group of plants. During this process, a pollen grain germinates on the stigma and forms a pollen tube, which extends down through the style to reach the ovule in the ovary. The generative cell within the pollen tube divides to form two male gametes (sperm cells).

Among these two male gametes, one fuses with the egg cell to form a diploid zygote, which will eventually develop into an embryo. The other male gamete fuses with the diploid secondary nucleus (which is formed by the fusion of the two polar nuclei in the central cell of the embryo sac) to form a triploid cell. This triploid cell develops into the endosperm, which will provide nourishment to the developing embryo.

Therefore, the correct function of these gametes is described by Option D. Here’s the explanation in a more summarized form:

Option D: One gamete fuses with the egg cell and the other one fuses with a diploid secondary nucleus.

In summary, double fertilization in angiosperms involves the following:

  1. One male gamete fuses with the egg cell to form the zygote (2n).

  1. The other male gamete fuses with the diploid secondary nucleus to form the triploid endosperm (3n).

This can be represented in the following manner using LaTeX:

$$ \text{First Fusion:} \quad Sperm (\text{n}) + Egg (\text{n}) \rightarrow Zygote (\text{2n}) $$

$$ \text{Second Fusion:} \quad Sperm (\text{n}) + Secondary Nucleus (\text{2n}) \rightarrow Endosperm (\text{3n}) $$

question: In a sexually reproducing organism, which one of the following statements is appropriate both for the parent and offspring?

en.content: In a sexually reproducing organism, which one of the following statements is appropriate both for the parent and offspring?

options.content: Chromosome number increases but DNA content remains constant
Both chromosome number and DNA content remains constant
Chromosome number decreases but DNA content remains constant
Both chromosome number and DNA content decreases

en.explanation:
In sexually reproducing organisms, the process of reproduction involves the combination of genetic material from two parents to produce offspring. This usually occurs through the formation of gametes (sperm and egg cells) via meiosis, followed by fertilization.

During meiosis, the chromosome number is halved, resulting in gametes that have half the number of chromosomes of the parent organism. In humans, for example, somatic (body) cells have 46 chromosomes, but gametes have 23 chromosomes. When these gametes combine during fertilization, the resulting zygote has the full set of chromosomes, i.e., 46 chromosomes (23 pairs).

Both the parent and offspring maintain a constant chromosome number due to these processes. The DNA content, however, varies through different stages of the cell cycle and meiosis. Yet, on a broader generational scale, the DNA content remains relatively constant from one generation to the next in terms of the total amount of genetic information passed down.

Thus, the appropriate statement for both the parent and the offspring in a sexually reproducing organism is:

Option B

Both chromosome number and DNA content remain constant.

This reflects the fact that the overall genetic material and chromosome number are maintained across generations, although there can be variations within different stages of cell development and fertilization processes.

In mathematical terms, the chromosome number of the parent ($$2n$$) will be halved during meiosis to produce gametes ($$n$$), and then restored to $$2n$$ in the offspring during fertilization:

$$ \text{Parent (2n)} \rightarrow \text{Meiosis} \rightarrow \text{Gametes (n)} \rightarrow \text{Fertilization} \rightarrow \text{Offspring (2n)} $$

question: Relationship between a flower and honey-bee will help the flower for/in

en.content: Relationship between a flower and honey-bee will help the flower for/in
en.explanation:
The correct answer is Option B: Pollination. Here's why:

The relationship between a flower and a honeybee is a classic example of mutualism, where both organisms benefit. Here's how it works:

* Honeybee's Perspective: Honeybees are attracted to flowers by their bright colors, sweet scents, and nectar. They collect nectar as a food source and pollen as a source of protein.

* Flower's Perspective: Flowers rely on honeybees (and other pollinators) to transfer pollen from the male part of the flower (anther) to the female part (stigma). This process, called pollination, is essential for the flower to produce seeds and reproduce.

Let's look at why the other options are incorrect:

Option A: Faster development: While pollination is necessary for seed development, it doesn't directly impact the flower's overall development speed.

Option C: Quick germination of pollen: Germination refers to the sprouting of seeds, not pollen. Pollen needs to be transferred to the stigma of the flower to initiate fertilization and subsequent seed development.

Option D: Increase in size: Pollination is about reproduction, not growth. A flower's size is determined by its genetic makeup and environmental factors.

In summary, the primary benefit a flower receives from its relationship with a honeybee is pollination, which is crucial for the flower's ability to produce seeds and continue its species.

options: CO2
O2
question: Which one of the following is NOT a requirement for photosynthesis?

en.content: Which one of the following is NOT a requirement for photosynthesis?

options.content: CO2
O2

en.explanation:
Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants, algae, and some bacteria convert light energy into chemical energy, storing it in the bonds of sugar. This process requires several key components:

1. Chlorophyll: This is the green pigment located in the chloroplasts of plant cells. It is essential because it absorbs light, usually sunlight, and converts it into chemical energy. Therefore, Chlorophyll is clearly required for photosynthesis (Option A).

2. Carbon Dioxide (CO2): Plants take in carbon dioxide from the atmosphere through small openings in their leaves known as stomata. Carbon dioxide is one of the raw materials used in the photosynthesis process to produce glucose. Thus, CO2 is also necessary (Option B).

3. Water (H2O): Water is absorbed by plant roots from the soil and is transported to the leaves where photosynthesis occurs. It is another raw material needed for the chemical reactions that produce glucose and oxygen. Hence, Water is another essential requirement (Option C).

4. Oxygen (O2): While oxygen is produced during photosynthesis, it is not a requirement for the process to occur. Oxygen is actually a by-product of the splitting of water molecules during the light-dependent reactions of photosynthesis. Therefore, Oxygen is not necessary for photosynthesis to take place (Option D).

From the given options, the correct answer is Option D: O2.

question: In flowering plants, DNA content of the parent plant gets halved during

en.content: In flowering plants, DNA content of the parent plant gets halved during
en.explanation:
In flowering plants, the DNA content of the parent plant gets halved during the formation of gametes through the process of meiosis. This reduction is crucial for sexual reproduction because it ensures that the offspring have the same amount of DNA as the parent plants. The halving of DNA typically occurs during the formation of pollen in male gametophytes and ovules in female gametophytes.

Let’s explore the options:

Option A: Seed germination

Seed germination is the process by which a seed develops into a new plant. This process does not involve the halving of DNA content, as it is primarily concerned with the growth and development of the embryo contained within the seed.

Option B: Fruit formation

Fruit formation occurs after fertilization. It is the development of the ovary into fruit, and this also does not involve the halving of DNA content. The DNA content in the cells of the fruit typically remains diploid.

Option C: Flower bud formation

Flower bud formation is the development of a bud into a flower, which eventually plays a role in reproduction. However, the halving of DNA does not occur at this stage; it takes place later during the formation of gametes.

Option D: Pollen formation

Pollen formation is part of the process of gametogenesis in plants, specifically the formation of male gametes. During this process, meiosis occurs, resulting in the halving of DNA content from diploid to haploid. This ensures that when fertilization occurs, the resulting zygote has a complete diploid set of chromosomes.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option D: Pollen formation

The reduction in DNA content happens during meiosis, particularly during the production of gametophytes, such as pollen in male plants. This reduction is critical for maintaining the appropriate chromosome number through successive generations.

question: Which one of the following is the correct sequence during sexual reproduction in Angiosperms?

en.content: Which one of the following is the correct sequence during sexual reproduction in Angiosperms?
en.explanation:
The correct sequence during sexual reproduction in Angiosperms is Option A: Egg → zygote → embryo → seed. Here's why:

1. Egg: The process begins with the female gamete, the egg (or ovum), produced within the ovary of the flower.

2. Zygote: During fertilization, the egg is fused with the male gamete, the sperm, which is delivered by a pollen tube. This fusion creates a diploid cell called the zygote. The zygote contains genetic material from both the male and female parents.

3. Embryo: The zygote undergoes multiple rounds of cell division and differentiation, eventually developing into an embryo. The embryo is the young, developing plant within the seed.

4. Seed: The embryo, along with the surrounding nutritive tissue (endosperm) and protective seed coat, forms the seed. The seed is essentially a package containing the potential for a new plant.

Let's look at why the other options are incorrect:

Option B: Embryo → egg → zygote → seed - This is incorrect because the embryo forms after the zygote.

Option C: Egg → embryo → zygote → seed - This is incorrect because the zygote is formed immediately after the egg is fertilized.

Option D: Egg → seed → zygote → embryo - This is incorrect because the seed is formed after the embryo develops.
